rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PHILIP M. EISENBERG TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Demurrer To Plaintiffs Corrected Petition To Invalidate Trust And Class Action For Damages
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 7, 2012, Line 1. RESPONDENT JAY ZALEWSKI Demurrer To Plaintiffs Corrected Petition To Invalidate Trust And Class Action For Damages. Sustained without leave to amend as to all causes of action alleged by Mr. Messick and as to the unjust enrichment cause of action and overruled in all other respects. Mr. Messick had a full opportunity to participate in the proceedings leading up to the September 2011 probate court order. Unjust enrichment is not a cause of action and restitution is an available remedy for the other causes of action. All causes of action other than unjust enrichment are adequately pled. Res judicata does not apply to bar the claims of the Regents or the Blind Babies Foundation since those plaintiffs were not parties to and did not receive notice of the probate proceedings. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL MCNAIR VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 7, 2012, Line 4. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, ANN KIM MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment is denied and alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the breach of contract claim and granted as to the intentional tort claim. Whether viewed as a separate contract or part of a larger contract between the parties, the HIPAA notice contains binding contractual obligations, the breach of which are subject to contract remedies, notwithstanding that there is no private right of action to enforce HIPAA. The contact claim is not barred by the Government claims rules. The argument that the contract claim is barred because the contract was not approved by the City Attorney is rejected because that was not a basis on which summary adjudication was sought, the argument was made for the first time in the reply papers and there is no mention of any facts supporting the argument in the moving separate statement. The intentional tort cause of action is barred by the litigation privilege. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CALVO & CLARK LLP, VS. DAVID J. LUJAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Calvo Fisher & Jacob Llp'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 7, 2012, Line 5. Plaintiff Calvo Fisher & Jacob Llp'S Motion For Summary Judgment. Motion for summary judgment is denied and the alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the breach of contract, fraud in the inducement, non-disclosure, breach of the implied covenant, and negligent misrepresentation claims and off calendar as to the malpractice and breach of fiduciary duty claims. There are triable issues regarding liability and damages on all of the contract and fraud claims. The malpractice and fiduciary duty claims are off calendar due to the ruling on the demurrer. The hearing will be at 10:30am, not 9:30am or 10am. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CALVO & CLARK LLP, VS. DAVID J. LUJAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 7, 2012, Line 5. CROSS DEFENDANT CALVO FISHER & JACOB LLP MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Denied in its entirety. The motion is not a proper use of the motion to strike procedure and instead, to a large extent, is a disguised motion for summary judgment or adjudication. In all events, the challenged allegations are adequately pled. The hearing will be at 10:30am, not 9:30am or 10am. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINE CHANG VS. JANE L. O'HARA GAMP
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Terminating Sanctions Of Dismissal With Prejudice, Striking The Complaint, Or Entering Default Judgment Against Pltf; Or, In The Alternative, 2) Compelling Pltf'S Compliance With The Court'S Order Granting Defts' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery And Imposition Of Monetary ($1,620), Issue, And/Or Evidence Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday Feb. 7, 2012 Line 2 - DEFENDANT JANE GAMP, SAN FRANCISCO LAW SCHOOL Notice Of Motion For Terminating Sanctions Of Dismissal With Prejudice, Striking The Complaint, Or Entering Default Judgment Against Pltf; Or, In The Alternative, 2) Compelling Pltf'S Compliance With The Court'S Order Granting Defts' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery And Imposition Of Monetary ($1,620), Issue, And/Or Evidence Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing.

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL E. BUTLER VS. LAUREEN PARAGUYA DR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 7, 2012, Line 13. DEFENDANT LAUREEN PARAGUYA DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. The plaintiff has alleged a discovery date for the alleged fraud that renders his fraud claim not time-barred. Plaintiff has also alleged an explanation why that date differs from the discovery date alleged in his prior complaint. That explanation, if true - and the court must assume that it is true for purposes of this demurrer - is sufficient to avoid the prior date from being binding on the plaintiff. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: FIONE SU VS. DUMPLING KITCHEN LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 7, 2012, Line 15. DEFENDANT DUMPLING KITCHEN LLC DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the Unruh Act claim, sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the negligence claim, and sustained without leave to amend as to the premises liability and intentional infliction claims. Plaintiff has adequately alleged an Unruh Act claim. Plaintiff is granted leave, if she can do so in good faith, to allege a claim for negligent hiring, supervision or training. A premises liability claim is not applicable in the circumstances alleged. The intentional infliction claim fails because the alleged misconduct is not beyond all possible bounds of decencyAny party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
